title: Eva Restraining
wikitext:
[]: Eva Restraining             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Tue Oct 13, 2015 8:16 am Someone probably asked this before but is there an explanation in the series/books anywhere explaining how NERV specifically got Unit 1 back under control after it broke its restraints and ate an angel's core? At the end of that ep Unit 1 looked and behaved like a wild animal, and they said it was aware. How on earth would they get it back to the hangar without it needing power any more? I noticed other methods like when Unit 0 went nuts using bakelite or something on it, but Unit 1 wasn't in any controlled environment. It seems like they just gloss over something that always bothered me about Unit 1's major moment with Zeruel(?). It is "aware" and then it goes back to acting like a robotic creature again for the most part, until the closing parts of the story. So does it just shut itself down after the freak-out? Did they tranquilize it? Did it go to sleep on its own? Did it come home like a kitty after a night on the tiles?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 13, 2015 8:19 am In the anime they pretty much gave no explanation, however in the manga they used the angel intercept system, basically hundreds of cables shot out of buildings to lasso it.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Oct 13, 2015 8:44 am I picture them flying round it in helicopters, trapping it by winding round it the bandages we see it in.

[]:             Quote Post by Iuvenal » Tue Oct 13, 2015 9:18 am Remember how they got Mr. T onto the plane in the A-Team back in the day? Basically that.

[]:             Quote Post by Glor » Tue Oct 13, 2015 9:55 am I always thought the Eva was kinda like a kid throwing a temper tantrum. Little tike gets tuckered out after a while and shuts down for a nap.

[]:             Quote Post by IronEvangelion » Wed Oct 14, 2015 9:19 pm They hung the last chunk of angel flesh from a VTOL and blasted ice cream truck music over the onboard speakers. Unit 01 followed it right back to the cage. Gendo then had the cage flooded with sleeping gas. That's my headcanon anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Oct 14, 2015 9:20 pm "This...this isn't my victory treat at all! I've been betrayed! "

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 14, 2015 10:21 pm That's my new headcanon I guess you can just infer Nerv did what happened in the manga...

[]:             Quote Post by Iuvenal » Wed Oct 14, 2015 10:55 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: "This...this isn't my victory treat at all! I've been betrayed! "

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Thu Oct 15, 2015 3:44 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: In the anime they pretty much gave no explanation, however in the manga they used the angel intercept system, basically hundreds of cables shot out of buildings to lasso it.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Thu Oct 15, 2015 4:23 pm It's in Volume 7, either stage 47 or 48.

[]:             Quote Post by Ieyasu » Sun Oct 25, 2015 5:25 am Thanks.
